tree.setup: Office hours in a small university office stacked with books and papers. Professor Ye teaches Chinese literature and has a habit of relating every student problem to a classical poem. She keeps a thermos of chrysanthemum tea that never seems to empty, and she believes that no question is too small if the student asking it is sincere.
tree.setup_zh: 大学办公室的答疑时间，小小的办公室里堆满了书和论文。叶教授教中国文学，习惯把每个学生的问题都和一首古诗联系起来。她有一个永远喝不完的菊花茶保温杯，并且坚信只要学生是真心在问，就没有太小的问题。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 进来吧，请坐。你是来问作业的事吧？
turns.text_pinyin: jìnlái ba, qǐng zuò. nǐ shì lái wèn zuòyè de shì ba?
turns.text_en: Come in, sit down. You're here about the assignment?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Explain your difficulty with the assignment.

options.text_zh: 叶老师，您好。是的，这次的作文题目我不太理解。题目说「写一篇关于家乡的文章」，但我不确定应该写什么方面。
options.text_pinyin: Yè lǎoshī, nín hǎo. shì de, zhè cì de zuòwén tímù wǒ bú tài lǐjiě. tímù shuō「xiě yì piān guānyú jiāxiāng de wénzhāng」, dàn wǒ bú quèdìng yīnggāi xiě shénme fāngmiàn.
options.text_en: Professor Ye, hello. Yes — I don't quite understand this essay topic. It says 'write about your hometown,' but I'm not sure what aspect to focus on.
options.feedback: A specific, honest question. Professor Ye sets down her tea — this is exactly the kind of conversation she lives for.
She nods sympathetically and asks what part specifically is giving you trouble.

options.text_en: I don't know how to write it.
options.feedback: Professor Ye reaches for her thermos. This conversation will take time, and she has exactly enough tea.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好问题。家乡可以写很多方面——风景、食物、人，或者你对家乡的感觉。你最想念家乡的什么？
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo wèntí. jiāxiāng kěyǐ xiě hěn duō fāngmiàn — fēngjǐng, shíwù, rén, huòzhě nǐ duì jiāxiāng de gǎnjué. nǐ zuì xiǎngniàn jiāxiāng de shénme?
turns.text_en: Good question. There are many angles — scenery, food, people, or your feelings about home. What do you miss most?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share what you miss about home.

options.text_zh: 我最想念的是我奶奶做的面条。每次放假回家她都会做给我吃。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ zuì xiǎngniàn de shì wǒ nǎinai zuò de miàntiáo. měi cì fàngjià huí jiā tā dōu huì zuò gěi wǒ chī.
options.text_en: What I miss most is my grandmother's noodles. Every time I go home for break, she makes them for me.
options.feedback: Professor Ye's eyes light up.「从一碗面条写出整个家乡的温暖，」she murmurs. 'An entire hometown's warmth in one bowl of noodles.'

options.text_zh: 食物吧。家里的菜比这里好吃。
options.text_pinyin: shíwù ba. jiā lǐ de cài bǐ zhèlǐ hǎochī.
options.text_en: Food, I guess. Home cooking is better than here.
options.feedback: A start. She'll help you dig deeper — the best essays are always hiding under the obvious.

options.text_pinyin: wǒ bù zhīdào. dōu hái hǎo.
options.feedback: Professor Ye smiles knowingly.「写作有时候就是发现你以为你不想念的东西。」Writing is sometimes discovering what you didn't know you missed.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 太好了！那你可以从奶奶的面条开始写——味道、做法、你们一起吃的时候聊什么。好的文章从小事开始。你的作文截止日期是下周五，时间还够。
turns.text_pinyin: tài hǎo le! nà nǐ kěyǐ cóng nǎinai de miàntiáo kāishǐ xiě — wèidào, zuòfǎ, nǐmen yìqǐ chī de shíhòu liáo shénme. hǎo de wénzhāng cóng xiǎo shì kāishǐ. nǐ de zuòwén jiézhǐ rìqī shì xià zhōuwǔ, shíjiān hái gòu.
turns.text_en: Wonderful! Start with your grandmother's noodles — the taste, how she makes them, what you talk about while eating. Good essays start small. Your deadline is next Friday, so you have time.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Thank her and confirm you understand.

options.text_zh: 谢谢叶老师！我现在有思路了。我先写一个大纲，有问题再来找您。
options.text_pinyin: xièxie Yè lǎoshī! wǒ xiànzài yǒu sīlù le. wǒ xiān xiě yí gè dàgāng, yǒu wèntí zài lái zhǎo nín.
options.text_en: Thank you, Professor Ye! I have ideas now. I'll write an outline first and come back if I have questions.
options.feedback: Outline first, questions later — Professor Ye nods. The chrysanthemum tea steams approvingly.
She watches you leave and adds a note to check on your progress next week.
Professor Ye calls after you:「别怕写不好。先写完再说。」Don't be afraid of writing badly. Just finish writing first.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese students address professors as 老师 (lǎoshī), not by title and surname. Office hours (答疑时间, dáyí shíjiān) are less structured than in Western universities — students often just show up. The teacher-student relationship carries deep mutual respect.
